name: Sourdough French Bread
recipeIngredient: 125 ml milk (any type, but I prefer whole milk)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine milk, sugar, salt, and shortening in a pan. Heat until the shortening melts, then cool to lukewarm (about 45°C).
2. Stir the milk mixture into the starter. Add flour gradually, forming dough that pulls away from the sides of the bowl.
3. Knead until smooth (about 15 minutes), adding flour as needed to keep the dough from sticking to the board. When done, the dough will be fairly heavy but smooth in texture.
4. Place in a well-greased bowl and raise in a warm place until doubled.
5. Form into a long loaf (about 40 cm long). Place on a greased baking sheet and let rise again until almost doubled.
6. Cut diagonal slits in the top of the loaf with a sharp knife, and bake at 400°F for about 30-35 minutes, or until done.
7. Remove from pan and let cool in a draft-free location.
Difficulty: moderate
Precision: measure ingredients
